nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; tends to be observed around isolated coral heads, reef slopes, and lagoon edges at depths of roughly 1–30 m

diet.notes: In the wild has been documented feeding on coral polyps, small crustaceans, tubeworms, and algae grazed from rocks. In captivity, generally advised to offer small frequent meals of mysis shrimp, vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, finely chopped marine meaty foods, plus spirulina, nori, and algae-based preparations 2–3 times daily. Food should be chopped to small sizes given the species' small mouth. New arrivals tend to acclimate more readily in well-established systems with natural algae growth.

avoidWith: other butterflyfish unless an established bonded pair

summary: An Indian Ocean butterflyfish with a pearl-white body bordered in golden-yellow and overlaid with grey-blue chevron markings, plus a yellow head and tail. Generally considered peaceful toward most fish but not reef safe, as it has been documented nipping corals and sessile invertebrates.
careNotes:
Chaetodon xanthocephalus is distributed across the Western and Eastern Indian Ocean from East Africa to Sri Lanka, the Maldives, and as far east as western Indonesia, where it tends to be observed around isolated coral heads on reef slopes and lagoons at depths of roughly 1–30 m. It is generally considered an intermediate-difficulty butterflyfish — hardy once established, but new arrivals can be slow to accept prepared foods and have been known to do best when introduced to mature systems with established algae growth and a varied small-meal feeding schedule.

This species is not reef safe. It has been documented feeding on stony and soft coral polyps, tubeworms, ornamental shrimp, and has been known to nip Tridacna clams. It is best housed in a fish-only-with-live-rock (FOWLR) or fish-only system with ample open swimming space, plenty of rock work for shelter, strong oxygenation, and excellent water quality.

It is generally observed as solitary in the wild, occasionally forming loose groups of 5–6, and has been reported to form monogamous pairs during breeding. In aquariums, only one specimen should be kept per tank unless a known bonded male/female pair is introduced simultaneously. Tankmates that are themselves butterflyfishes should generally be avoided.

Source cross-verification notes: Adult max size is consistent at ~20 cm across FishBase, ReefApp, and Fishkeeper (LiveAquaria reports 8 in / 20.3 cm — agrees). Tank-size recommendations ranged from ~380 L (100 gal, some retailers) through 450 L (Recifathome) and 473 L (LiveAquaria 125 gal) to 500 L (ReefApp); biased to the larger end (500 L) given prose context citing active swimming, ample open space, and need for a mature system. Temperature range: LiveAquaria 22–25.6°C, FishKeeper 23–27°C, FishBase wild 25.9–29.3°C; averaged to 23–27°C. Salinity: LiveAquaria and FishKeeper 1.020–1.025, ReefApp 1.026; widened to 1.020–1.026. Care level: LiveAquaria 'Moderate', FishKeeper 'Advanced'; resolved to intermediate as the middle value. Reef Safe: 'No' confirmed by all sources.
breedingNotes: Has been documented as monogamous and oviparous with pelagic larvae. Like most butterflyfishes, successful captive rearing of fry is rarely if ever achieved due to the difficulty of culturing the planktonic larval stage. Generally considered not bred in captivity for practical purposes. Hybrids with C. ephippium have been reported in the wild.

primary.name: LiveAquaria
primary.notes: Primary husbandry source: max size 8 in, min tank 125 gal, temperament peaceful (territorial toward other butterflyfish), reef safe no, temperature 72–78°F, pH 8.1–8.4, SG 1.020–1.025, dKH 8–12, care level Moderate.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Used in place of the assigned Reef2Reef thread (HTTP 403). Confirmed taxonomy (Chaetodontidae, Acanthuriformes), max length 20 cm TL, depth 1–30 m, native distribution (Western Indian Ocean: East Africa to Sri Lanka and the Maldives), monogamous/oviparous reproduction, territorial/aggressive behavior toward other butterflyfish, hybridization with C. ephippium.

additional.name: ReefApp Encyclopedia
additional.notes: Cross-checked tank size (500 L), max size (20 cm), salinity (SG 1.026), reef-safe status (no — eats tubeworms, nips Tridacna), diet (varied carnivorous + algae grazing), behavior (active swimmer, peaceful to other fish).

additional.name: Fishkeeper.co.uk
additional.notes: Cross-checked max size (20 cm), temperature (23–27°C), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), care level ('Advanced'), reef status (avoid stony corals/sessile inverts), feeding approach (small meals, finely chopped). Recommended specimen size ~10 cm for acclimation.

additional.name: Recifathome
additional.notes: Cross-checked minimum tank volume (450 L), described as 'discreet and peaceful' preferring solitary keeping, 20 cm adult size. Used to confirm tank-size lower bound.

fish.breedingNotes: Pelagic larvae are extremely difficult to raise; captive breeding to successful fry stage has rarely if ever been achieved. Forms monogamous pairs in the wild.
